commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex plugin config for selectable workspace prompt routing and native personality mode, updates app-server binding metadata/fingerprints, keeps Codex project docs native, and documents the new prompt-surface behavior.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature/config PR rather than a reported current-main bug. Source inspection and branch tests cover the intended paths, but I did not establish a real app-server rotation reproduction outside the provided proof.

target.title: [codex] make workspace prompt routing selectable
target.body:
## Summary This rebuild turns the Codex workspace prompt routing from a forced token-efficiency change into an explicit, user-selectable prompt-surface policy. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.workspacePromptSurface`: - `per_turn_context` (default): quality-first legacy behavior. `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`, `MEMORY.md`, and active `BOOTSTRAP.md` remain fresh current-turn context. - `thread_developer`: opt-in token-efficient/high-priority mode. Only stable profile files (`SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`) move to Codex thread developer instructions. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.personalityMode`: - `soul_when_present` (default): when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered, send Codex `personality: "none"` and let the OpenClaw bridge point personality/tone/voice at `SOUL.md`. - `codex`: keep Codex's native personality overlay. - `none`: always disable Codex's named native personality overlay. - Keeps `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` native to Codex project-doc loading instead of duplicating them in OpenClaw prompt text, while fingerprinting their root workspace content so existing OpenClaw bindings rotate when the docs change. - Removes the lightweight Codex `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression so cron/lightweight Codex runs preserve exact user input without disabling native project docs. Lightweight exact-command runs also do not disable Codex's native personality just because `SOUL.md` exists on disk; the SOUL override applies only when SOUL context is actually delivered. - Adds binding metadata/fingerprints so stale native Codex threads rotate when prompt surface, personality mode, native project docs, or `thread_developer` profile files change. ## Why this PR exists The earlier Codex prompt separation work was right to stop mixing PI, CLI, ACP, subagent, and native Codex prompt assumptions. Native Codex should keep its own base/model contract, while OpenClaw should add only the host-runtime bridge it owns: message delivery, dynamic OpenClaw tools, ACP/OpenClaw delegation boundaries, and runtime/channel context. The next optimization moved `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `USER.md` out of every turn and into Codex developer instructions. That was attractive for token efficiency, but the adversarial review found a real quality/regression risk: users edit soul/profile/memory files during long-running OpenClaw sessions, and native Codex thread developer instructions are not a per-turn live-reload surface. Once a native Codex thread is already bound, resume-time developer/personality changes can be ignored by the loaded thread unless OpenClaw intentionally rotates the binding. The final direction here is: quality is the default, token efficiency is a choice. ## Prompt shape ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Codex base/model instructions"] --> B["Codex native personality slot"] B --> C["OpenClaw Codex developer bridge"] C --> D{"workspacePromptSurface"} D -->|"per_turn_context (default)"| E["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
MEMORY / active BOOTSTRAP
fresh current-turn context"] D -->|"thread_developer (opt-in)"| F["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
Codex thread developer instructions"] E --> G["Current user request"] F --> G H["AGENTS.md / AGENTS.override.md"] --> I["Codex native project-doc loader"] I --> G J["HEARTBEAT.md"] --> K["Heartbeat collaboration-mode pointer"] K --> G ``` The bridge text is intentionally narrow. It says OpenClaw is the host runtime and Codex is the native execution harness, that OpenClaw may prepend current-turn workspace/memory/skill/channel context, and that this context is reference material for the current turn rather than a replacement for Codex system/developer instructions. It also explicitly avoids treating every OpenClaw turn as coding work. When non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered and `personalityMode` is left at the default, Codex receives the protocol enum `personality: "none"`. This is not model-visible text that says "have no personality"; Codex's protocol describes `none` as an empty native personality placeholder. The positive personality source remains `SOUL.md` via the OpenClaw bridge. Empty placeholder SOUL files keep Codex's native personality behavior. ## What we learned ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Original legacy behavior
fresh per-turn soul/profile context"] --> B["Prompt separation
Codex gets Codex lane, PI gets PI lane"] B --> C["Token optimization
stable files moved to developerInstructions"] C --> D["Adversarial finding
developerInstructions are not reliable live-refresh for existing native threads"] D --> E["This PR
default legacy quality + opt-in thread developer mode + binding rotation"] ``` Key findings from the review: - `SOUL.md` is personality, not collaboration mode. `collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` is too strong and mode-oriented for general soul/profile content; it remains appropriate for heartbeat/cron pointer behavior. - `AGENTS.md` and `AGENTS.override.md` are Codex project-doc concerns. OpenClaw should not duplicate them into turn text, but it should stop suppressing native loading and rotate OpenClaw's native Codex binding when loaded project-doc content changes. - `MEMORY.md`, active `BOOTSTRAP.md`, and skills are volatile enough to remain per-turn context even in the token-efficient mode. - `thread_developer` needs freshness metadata. If users opt into it, profile-file edits must rotate the native Codex thread rather than silently relying on resume-time overrides. - The default should preserve the legacy quality/freshness behavior because token savings should not silently outweigh agent identity quality. OpenAI's Codex prompt write-up describes native project-doc loading with `AGENTS.override.md`, `AGENTS.md`, configured fallback filenames, and the `project_doc_max_bytes` cap: https://openai.com/index/unrolling-the-codex-agent-loop/ ## Authority and lifetime | Surface | Owner | Files / content | Lifetime | This PR | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Codex base/model instructions | Codex | Codex system/model stack | Native thread | OpenClaw does not override | | Codex personality slot | Codex protocol | `friendly`, `pragmatic`, `none` | Native thread/turn param | Configurable via `personalityMode`; defaults to `none` only when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered | | OpenClaw developer bridge | OpenClaw | Runtime/delivery/tool/delegation bridge | Native thread | Kept narrow and host-owned | | OpenClaw per-turn context | OpenClaw | Soul/profile/memory/bootstrap/skills/channel context | Every turn | Default quality-first surface | | Codex thread developer instructions | OpenClaw -> Codex | Stable soul/profile files only | Native thread | Opt-in `thread_developer`, with fingerprint rotation | | Native Codex project docs | Codex | `AGENTS.md`, `AGENTS.override.md`, fallbacks | Native project-doc load | Preserved; no `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression | | Heartbeat collaboration pointer | OpenClaw | Pointer to `HEARTBEAT.md` | Heartbeat turn | Still separate from soul/profile | ## Scenario coverage | Scenario | Expected behavior | | --- | --- | | Default Codex app-server run with non-empty `SOUL.md` | Stable profile files are current-turn context; Codex native personality is disabled; bridge points personality at `SOUL.md`. | | Default Codex app-server run with empty `SOUL.md` | Empty placeholder content does not suppress Codex's native personality overlay. | | User edits `SOUL.md` mid-session in default mode | Next turn sees the edit because the file remains per-turn context. | | User opts into `thread_developer` | Stable profile files move to developer instructions; `MEMORY.md` and active `BOOTSTRAP.md` remain turn context. | | User edits `SOUL.md` in `thread_developer` mode | OpenClaw fingerprints the tracked developer files and starts a fresh native Codex thread. | | User edits `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` | OpenClaw fingerprints loaded native project-doc content and rotates the native Codex binding. | | Lightweight cron Codex run | OpenClaw still skips extra bootstrap prompt text for exact command preservation, does not disable native Codex project docs, and does not send `personality: "none"` unless the user explicitly configured `personalityMode: "none"`. | | Existing stale binding from the previous PR | Missing prompt-surface metadata forces a new native Codex thread, so old bindings do not preserve stale developer/user context. | | Manual `/codex resume ` binding | The minimal manual binding resumes once, then persists the current prompt-surface/personality metadata after successful resume. | ## Implementation notes - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts` adds the new config enums and resolvers. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` selects the workspace prompt surface, computes the workspace fingerprint, chooses the positive SOUL bridge behavior, and keeps native project docs out of OpenClaw turn text. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` carries `personality`, `workspacePromptSurface`, and `workspacePromptFingerprint` through start/resume params and binding invalidation. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.ts` persists the prompt-surface metadata. - Docs now describe the architecture in `docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md`, `docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md`, and `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md`. ## Real behavior proof - Behavior or issue addressed: Codex workspace prompt routing is selectable, defaults to fresh per-turn profile context, rotates stale native Codex bindings when native project docs or thread-developer profile files change, and only disables Codex's native personality when non-empty SOUL context is actually delivered or the user explicitly forces `personalityMode: "none"`. - Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS under `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-codex-workspace-routing`, branch `codex/workspace-context-routing-v2`, head `342c00b220`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ -t "AGENTS.override|workspace prompt|thread-developer|lightweight cron|empty SOUL|native AGENTS.md" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts git diff --check pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { execSync } from "node:child_process"; import { resolveCodexPersonalityOverride, resolveCodexWorkspacePromptSurface, readCodexPluginConfig } from "./extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts"; const defaultConfig = readCodexPluginConfig({}); const threadConfig = readCodexPluginConfig({ workspacePromptSurface: "thread_developer", personalityMode: "soul_when_present" }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ proof: "openclaw codex workspace prompt routing", head: execSync("git rev-parse --short HEAD").toString().trim(), defaultSurface: resolveCodexWorkspacePromptSurface(defaultConfig), threadSurface: resolveCodexWorkspacePromptSurface(threadConfig), defaultWithDeliveredSoul: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: defaultConfig, hasSoulContext: true }) ?? "native-codex", defaultWithoutDeliveredSoul: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: defaultConfig, hasSoulContext: false }) ?? "native-codex", forcedNoneWithoutDeliveredSoul: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: readCodexPluginConfig({ personalityMode: "none" }), hasSoulContext: false }) ?? "native-codex" }, null, 2));' ``` - Evidence after fix (terminal capture / copied live output): ```text run-attempt focused app-server path: Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 6 passed | 186 skipped (192) thread-lifecycle app-server path: Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 36 passed (36) { "proof": "openclaw codex workspace prompt routing", "head": "342c00b220", "defaultSurface": "per_turn_context", "threadSurface": "thread_developer", "defaultWithDeliveredSoul": "none", "defaultWithoutDeliveredSoul": "native-codex", "forcedNoneWithoutDeliveredSoul": "none" } ``` - Observed result after fix: The focused app-server tests exercise `runCodexAppServerAttempt` request construction against the mock Codex app-server. They prove default per-turn routing, opt-in `thread_developer` routing, lightweight cron behavior, empty-SOUL native personality preservation, `AGENTS.md` rotation, and `AGENTS.override.md` rotation. The helper proof confirms the config resolver maps default/opt-in surfaces and personality overrides as expected. - What was not tested: A live networked model turn against a real Codex app-server session was not run locally; focused app-server tests exercise the OpenClaw request construction path without sending a model request. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper flagged prior heads because lightweight cron could see `SOUL.md` during bootstrap discovery, suppress actual OpenClaw context for exact-command mode, and still send `personality: "none"`; then because `AGENTS.override.md` was named as native but not independently loaded into the workspace prompt fingerprint. ## Validation Local validation was run from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-codex-workspace-routing` with worker-limited focused tests: ```bash OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts ``` Result: 5 files passed, 292 tests passed. Additional validation: ```bash OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts pnpm exec oxfmt --check \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts git diff --check pnpm check:changed ``` Result: passed. The three-file compatibility sweep passed 236 tests, and `pnpm check:changed` expanded to all lanes and completed typecheck, lint, guard checks, and runtime import-cycle checks. Latest review-fix validation on head `342c00b220`: ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ -t "AGENTS.override|workspace prompt|thread-developer|lightweight cron|empty SOUL|native AGENTS.md" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts git diff --check pnpm check:changed ``` Result: passed. The filtered run-attempt app-server path passed 6 targeted tests, thread lifecycle passed 36 tests, `git diff --check` passed, and `pnpm check:changed` completed successfully. ## Latest review fix: native project-doc selection Head `763b9a71f7` addresses the fresh ClawSweeper finding that the workspace fingerprint was hashing root `AGENTS.md` and `AGENTS.override.md` as fixed files instead of mirroring Codex's actual native project-doc selection. What changed: - The fingerprint now follows Codex's candidate order: `AGENTS.override.md`, then `AGENTS.md`, then configured `project_doc_fallback_filenames`. - It walks from the detected project root to the cwd and selects only the first existing candidate per directory, matching Codex's project-doc source list shape. - It honors `project_doc_max_bytes = 0` by not fingerprinting native project docs when Codex would not load them. - Configured fallback project docs are fingerprinted, excluded from duplicate OpenClaw per-turn prompt injection when selected natively, and counted as native project-doc injection in prompt reports. - New regressions cover: active `AGENTS.override.md` shadowing inactive `AGENTS.md`, configured fallback doc rotation, and root `AGENTS.md` rotation from a nested workspace. Focused validation on head `763b9a71f7`: ```bash OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ -t "AGENTS.override|workspace prompt|thread-developer|lightweight cron|empty SOUL|native AGENTS.md|fallback" OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts git diff --check pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts pnpm check:changed ``` Result: ```text run-attempt focused app-server path: Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 11 passed | 184 skipped (195) thread-lifecycle app-server path: Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 36 passed (36) pnpm check:changed: completed successfully; typecheck, lint, guard checks, and runtime import-cycle checks passed. ``` Direct local Codex app-server protocol probe: ```text which codex && codex --version /opt/homebrew/bin/codex codex-cli 0.128.0 ``` A no-model local `codex app-server --listen stdio://` probe accepted `thread/start` with OpenClaw-style `developerInstructions` and `personality: "none"`, and reported only the active override as the instruction source when both project-doc files existed: ```json { "proof": "direct Codex app-server offline start/resume probe", "codexCli": "0.128.0", "root": "", "threadStart": { "accepted": true, "threadId": "", "cwdMatches": true, "model": "gpt-5.4-codex", "modelProvider": "openai", "instructionSources": [ "/repo/AGENTS.override.md" ], "personalitySent": "none", "developerInstructionsCharsSent": 38 }, "threadResume": { "accepted": false, "offlineReason": "no rollout found for thread id " }, "notifications": [ "remoteControl/status/changed", "thread/started" ] } ``` Important boundary on that direct probe: offline `thread/resume` cannot succeed immediately after `thread/start` without a rollout from an actual turn, and I did not run a live networked model turn locally. The start proof is included to show the real Codex app-server protocol accepts the fields this PR sends and that native Codex selected `AGENTS.override.md` rather than the inactive `AGENTS.md`; binding start/resume rotation remains covered by the focused OpenClaw app-server path tests above. ## Latest review fix: project-doc byte budget Head `bd8b122de5` addresses the fresh ClawSweeper finding that selected native Codex project docs were fingerprinted in full even when Codex would only expose the first `project_doc_max_bytes` bytes. What changed: - Native project-doc fingerprinting now applies the same cumulative byte budget Codex applies before constructing model-visible project-doc instructions. - Out-of-budget edits in `AGENTS.md`, `AGENTS.override.md`, or configured fallback docs no longer rotate an existing native Codex binding when Codex-visible project-doc text is unchanged. - In-budget edits still rotate the binding, so actual visible instruction changes do not stay attached to a stale native thread. - The implementation was checked against Codex's native `agents_md.rs` behavior: skip when `project_doc_max_bytes` is zero, walk selected docs in project-root-to-cwd order, truncate bytes before UTF-8 lossy decode, skip whitespace-only visible docs, and subtract only visible non-empty bytes from the remaining budget. - New regressions cover both sides of the boundary: hidden out-of-budget project-doc edits resume the existing binding, while visible in-budget edits start a fresh native Codex thread. Focused validation on head `bd8b122de5`: ```bash OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ -t "AGENTS.override|fallback|project_doc_max_bytes|workspace prompt" OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts git diff --check pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts pnpm check:changed ``` Result: ```text run-attempt focused app-server path: Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 8 passed | 189 skipped (197) thread-lifecycle app-server path: Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 36 passed (36) formatting / whitespace: git diff --check passed; oxfmt --check passed. pnpm check:changed: completed successfully; typecheck, lint, guard checks, and runtime import-cycle checks passed. ``` Proof note: the two new `runCodexAppServerAttempt` regressions exercise the OpenClaw app-server binding path directly. They assert the actual `thread/resume` vs `thread/start` decision after project-doc edits with `project_doc_max_bytes = 8`: an out-of-budget edit resumes the existing binding and keeps the workspace prompt fingerprint stable; an in-budget edit starts a fresh native Codex thread and changes the fingerprint. The direct local Codex app-server protocol probe from the previous section still covers native field acceptance and source selection; I did not run a live networked model turn locally.

target.title: [codex] make workspace prompt routing selectable

Only stable profile files (`SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`) move to Codex thread developer instructions. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.personalityMode`: - `soul_when_present` (default): when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered, send Codex `personality: "none"` and let the OpenClaw bridge point personality/tone/voice at `SOUL.md`. - `codex`: keep Codex's native personality overlay. - `none`: always disable Codex's named native personality overlay. - Keeps `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` native to Codex project-doc loading instead of duplicating them in OpenClaw prompt text, while fingerprinting their root workspace content so existing OpenClaw bindings rotate when the docs change. - Removes the lightweight Codex `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression so cron/lightweight Codex runs preserve exact user input without disabling native project docs. Lightweight exact-command runs also do not disable Codex's native personality just because `SOUL.md` exists on disk; the SOUL override applies only when SOUL context is actually delivered. - Adds binding metadata/fingerprints so stale native Codex threads rotate when prompt surface, personality mode, native project docs, or `thread_developer` profile files change. ## Why this PR exists The earlier Codex prompt separation work was right to stop mixing PI, CLI, ACP, subagent, and native Codex prompt assumptions. Native Codex should keep its own base/model contract, while OpenClaw should add only the host-runtime bridge it owns: message delivery, dynamic OpenClaw tools, ACP/OpenClaw delegation boundaries, and runtime/channel context. The next optimization moved `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `USER.md` out of every turn and into Codex developer instructions. That was attractive for token efficiency, but the adversarial review found a real quality/regression risk: users edit soul/profile/memory files during long-running OpenClaw sessions, and native Codex thread developer instructions are not a per-turn live-reload surface. Once a native Codex thread is already bound, resume-time developer/personality changes can be ignored by the loaded thread unless OpenClaw intentionally rotates the binding. The final direction here is: quality is the default, token efficiency is a choice. ## Prompt shape ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Codex base/model instructions"] --> B["Codex native personality slot"] B --> C["OpenClaw Codex developer bridge"] C --> D{"workspacePromptSurface"} D -->|"per_turn_context (default)"| E["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
MEMORY / active BOOTSTRAP
fresh current-turn context"] D -->|"thread_developer (opt-in)"| F["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
Codex thread developer instructions"] E --> G["Current user request"] F --> G H["AGENTS.md / AGENTS.override.md"] --> I["Codex native project-doc loader"] I --> G J["HEARTBEAT.md"] --> K["Heartbeat collaboration-mode pointer"] K --> G ``` The bridge text is intentionally narrow. It says OpenClaw is the host runtime and Codex is the native execution harness, that OpenClaw may prepend current-turn workspace/memory/skill/channel context, and that this context is reference material for the current turn rather than a replacement for Codex system/developer instructions. It also explicitly avoids treating every OpenClaw turn as coding work. When non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered and `personalityMode` is left at the default, Codex receives the protocol enum `personality: "none"`. This is not model-visible text that says "have no personality"; Codex's protocol describes `none` as an empty native personality placeholder. The positive personality source remains `SOUL.md` via the OpenClaw bridge. Empty placeholder SOUL files keep Codex's native personality behavior. ## What we learned ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Original legacy behavior
fresh per-turn soul/profile context"] --> B["Prompt separation
Codex gets Codex lane, PI gets PI lane"] B --> C["Token optimization
stable files moved to developerInstructions"] C --> D["Adversarial finding
developerInstructions are not reliable live-refresh for existing native threads"] D --> E["This PR
default legacy quality + opt-in thread developer mode + binding rotation"] ``` Key findings from the review: - `SOUL.md` is personality, not collaboration mode. `collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` is too strong and mode-oriented for general soul/profile content; it remains appropriate for heartbeat/cron pointer behavior. - `AGENTS.md` and `AGENTS.override.md` are Codex project-doc concerns. OpenClaw should not duplicate them into turn text, but it should stop suppressing native loading and rotate OpenClaw's native Codex binding when loaded project-doc content changes. - `MEMORY.md`, active `BOOTSTRAP.md`, and skills are volatile enough to remain per-turn context even in the token-efficient mode. - `thread_developer` needs freshness metadata. If users opt into it, profile-file edits must rotate the native Codex thread rather than silently relying on resume-time overrides. - The default should preserve the legacy quality/freshness behavior because token savings should not silently outweigh agent identity quality. target.title: [codex] make workspace prompt routing selectable
target.body:
## Summary This rebuild turns the Codex workspace prompt routing from a forced token-efficiency change into an explicit, user-selectable prompt-surface policy. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.workspacePromptSurface`: - `per_turn_context` (default): quality-first legacy behavior. `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`, `MEMORY.md`, and active `BOOTSTRAP.md` remain fresh current-turn context. - `thread_developer`: opt-in token-efficient/high-priority mode. Only stable profile files (`SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`) move to Codex thread developer instructions. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.personalityMode`: - `soul_when_present` (default): when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered, send Codex `personality: "none"` and let the OpenClaw bridge point personality/tone/voice at `SOUL.md`. - `codex`: keep Codex's native personality overlay. - `none`: always disable Codex's named native personality overlay. - Keeps `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` native to Codex project-doc loading instead of duplicating them in OpenClaw prompt text, while fingerprinting their root workspace content so existing OpenClaw bindings rotate when the docs change. - Removes the lightweight Codex `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression so cron/lightweight Codex runs preserve exact user input without disabling native project docs. Lightweight exact-command runs also do not disable Codex's native personality just because `SOUL.md` exists on disk; the SOUL override applies only when SOUL context is actually delivered. - Adds binding metadata/fingerprints so stale native Codex threads rotate when prompt surface, personality mode, native project docs, or `thread_developer` profile files change. ## Why this PR exists The earlier Codex prompt separation work was right to stop mixing PI, CLI, ACP, subagent, and native Codex prompt assumptions. Native Codex should keep its own base/model contract, while OpenClaw should add only the host-runtime bridge it owns: message delivery, dynamic OpenClaw tools, ACP/OpenClaw delegation boundaries, and runtime/channel context. The next optimization moved `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `USER.md` out of every turn and into Codex developer instructions. That was attractive for token efficiency, but the adversarial review found a real quality/regression risk: users edit soul/profile/memory files during long-running OpenClaw sessions, and native Codex thread developer instructions are not a per-turn live-reload surface. Once a native Codex thread is already bound, resume-time developer/personality changes can be ignored by the loaded thread unless OpenClaw intentionally rotates the binding. The final direction here is: quality is the default, token efficiency is a choice. ## Prompt shape ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Codex base/model instructions"] --> B["Codex native personality slot"] B --> C["OpenClaw Codex developer bridge"] C --> D{"workspacePromptSurface"} D -->|"per_turn_context (default)"| E["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
MEMORY / active BOOTSTRAP
fresh current-turn context"] D -->|"thread_developer (opt-in)"| F["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
Codex thread developer instructions"] E --> G["Current user request"] F --> G H["AGENTS.md / AGENTS.override.md"] --> I["Codex native project-doc loader"] I --> G J["HEARTBEAT.md"] --> K["Heartbeat collaboration-mode pointer"] K --> G ``` The bridge text is intentionally narrow. It says OpenClaw is the host runtime and Codex is the native execution harness, that OpenClaw may prepend current-turn workspace/memory/skill/channel context, and that this context is reference material for the current turn rather than a replacement for Codex system/developer instructions. It also explicitly avoids treating every OpenClaw turn as coding work. When non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered and `personalityMode` is left at the default, Codex receives the protocol enum `personality: "none"`. This is not model-visible text that says "have no personality"; Codex's protocol describes `none` as an empty native personality placeholder. The positive personality source remains `SOUL.md` via the OpenClaw bridge. Empty placeholder SOUL files keep Codex's native personality behavior. ## What we learned ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Original legacy behavior
fresh per-turn soul/profile context"] --> B["Prompt separation
Codex gets Codex lane, PI gets PI lane"] B --> C["Token optimization
stable files moved to developerInstructions"] C --> D["Adversarial finding
developerInstructions are not reliable live-refresh for existing native threads"] D --> E["This PR
default legacy quality + opt-in thread developer mode + binding rotation"] ``` Key findings from the review: - `SOUL.md` is personality, not collaboration mode. `collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` is too strong and mode-oriented for general soul/profile content; it remains appropriate for heartbeat/cron pointer behavior. - `AGENTS.md` and `AGENTS.override.md` are Codex project-doc concerns. OpenClaw should not duplicate them into turn text, but it should stop suppressing native loading and rotate OpenClaw's native Codex binding when loaded project-doc content changes. - `MEMORY.md`, active `BOOTSTRAP.md`, and skills are volatile enough to remain per-turn context even in the token-efficient mode. - `thread_developer` needs freshness metadata. If users opt into it, profile-file edits must rotate the native Codex thread rather than silently relying on resume-time overrides. - The default should preserve the legacy quality/freshness behavior because token savings should not silently outweigh agent identity quality. OpenAI's Codex prompt write-up describes native project-doc loading with `AGENTS.override.md`, `AGENTS.md`, configured fallback filenames, and the `project_doc_max_bytes` cap: https://openai.com/index/unrolling-the-codex-agent-loop/ ## Authority and lifetime | Surface | Owner | Files / content | Lifetime | This PR | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Codex base/model instructions | Codex | Codex system/model stack | Native thread | OpenClaw does not override | | Codex personality slot | Codex protocol | `friendly`, `pragmatic`, `none` | Native thread/turn param | Configurable via `personalityMode`; defaults to `none` only when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered | | OpenClaw developer bridge | OpenClaw | Runtime/delivery/tool/delegation bridge | Native thread | Kept narrow and host-owned | | OpenClaw per-turn context | OpenClaw | Soul/profile/memory/bootstrap/skills/channel context | Every turn | Default quality-first surface | | Codex thread developer instructions | OpenClaw -> Codex | Stable soul/profile files only | Native thread | Opt-in `thread_developer`, with fingerprint rotation | | Native Codex project docs | Codex | `AGENTS.md`, `AGENTS.override.md`, fallbacks | Native project-doc load | Preserved; no `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression | | Heartbeat collaboration pointer | OpenClaw | Pointer to `HEARTBEAT.md` | Heartbeat turn | Still separate from soul/profile | ## Scenario coverage | Scenario | Expected behavior | | --- | --- | | Default Codex app-server run with non-empty `SOUL.md` | Stable profile files are current-turn context; Codex native personality is disabled; bridge points personality at `SOUL.md`. | | Default Codex app-server run with empty `SOUL.md` | Empty placeholder content does not suppress Codex's native personality overlay. | | User edits `SOUL.md` mid-session in default mode | Next turn sees the edit because the file remains per-turn context. | | User opts into `thread_developer` | Stable profile files move to developer instructions; `MEMORY.md` and active `BOOTSTRAP.md` remain turn context. | | User edits `SOUL.md` in `thread_developer` mode | OpenClaw fingerprints the tracked developer files and starts a fresh native Codex thread. | | User edits `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` | OpenClaw fingerprints loaded native project-doc content and rotates the native Codex binding. | | Lightweight cron Codex run | OpenClaw still skips extra bootstrap prompt text for exact command preservation, does not disable native Codex project docs, and does not send `personality: "none"` unless the user explicitly configured `personalityMode: "none"`. | | Existing stale binding from the previous PR | Missing prompt-surface metadata forces a new native Codex thread, so old bindings do not preserve stale developer/user context. | | Manual `/codex resume ` binding | The minimal manual binding resumes once, then persists the current prompt-surface/personality metadata after successful resume. | ## Implementation notes - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts` adds the new config enums and resolvers. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` selects the workspace prompt surface, computes the workspace fingerprint, chooses the positive SOUL bridge behavior, and keeps native project docs out of OpenClaw turn text. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` carries `personality`, `workspacePromptSurface`, and `workspacePromptFingerprint` through start/resume params and binding invalidation. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.ts` persists the prompt-surface metadata. - Docs now describe the architecture in `docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md`, `docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md`, and `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md`. ## Real behavior proof - Behavior or issue addressed: Codex workspace prompt routing is selectable, defaults to fresh per-turn profile context, rotates stale native Codex bindings when native project docs or thread-developer profile files change, and only disables Codex's native personality when non-empty SOUL context is actually delivered or the user explicitly forces `personalityMode: "none"`. - Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS under `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-codex-workspace-routing`, branch `codex/workspace-context-routing-v2`, head `342c00b220`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ -t "AGENTS.override|workspace prompt|thread-developer|lightweight cron|empty SOUL|native AGENTS.md" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts git diff --check pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { execSync } from "node:child_process"; import { resolveCodexPersonalityOverride, resolveCodexWorkspacePromptSurface, readCodexPluginConfig } from "./extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts"; const defaultConfig = readCodexPluginConfig({}); const threadConfig = readCodexPluginConfig({ workspacePromptSurface: "thread_developer", personalityMode: "soul_when_present" }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ proof: "openclaw codex workspace prompt routing", head: execSync("git rev-parse --short HEAD").toString().trim(), defaultSurface: resolveCodexWorkspacePromptSurface(defaultConfig), threadSurface: resolveCodexWorkspacePromptSurface(threadConfig), defaultWithDeliveredSoul: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: defaultConfig, hasSoulContext: true }) ?? "native-codex", defaultWithoutDeliveredSoul: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: defaultConfig, hasSoulContext: false }) ?? "native-codex", forcedNoneWithoutDeliveredSoul: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: readCodexPluginConfig({ personalityMode: "none" }), hasSoulContext: false }) ?? "native-codex" }, null, 2));' ``` - Evidence after fix (terminal capture / copied live output): ```text run-attempt focused app-server path: Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 6 passed | 186 skipped (192) thread-lifecycle app-server path: Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 36 passed (36) { "proof": "openclaw codex workspace prompt routing", "head": "342c00b220", "defaultSurface": "per_turn_context", "threadSurface": "thread_developer", "defaultWithDeliveredSoul": "none", "defaultWithoutDeliveredSoul": "native-codex", "forcedNoneWithoutDeliveredSoul": "none" } ``` - Observed result after fix: The focused app-server tests exercise `runCodexAppServerAttempt` request construction against the mock Codex app-server. They prove default per-turn routing, opt-in `thread_developer` routing, lightweight cron behavior, empty-SOUL native personality preservation, `AGENTS.md` rotation, and `AGENTS.override.md` rotation. target.title: [codex] make workspace prompt routing selectable
target.body:
## Summary This rebuild turns the Codex workspace prompt routing from a forced token-efficiency change into an explicit, user-selectable prompt-surface policy. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.workspacePromptSurface`: - `per_turn_context` (default): quality-first legacy behavior. `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`, `MEMORY.md`, and active `BOOTSTRAP.md` remain fresh current-turn context. - `thread_developer`: opt-in token-efficient/high-priority mode. Only stable profile files (`SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`) move to Codex thread developer instructions. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.personalityMode`: - `soul_when_present` (default): when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered, send Codex `personality: "none"` and let the OpenClaw bridge point personality/tone/voice at `SOUL.md`. - `codex`: keep Codex's native personality overlay. - `none`: always disable Codex's named native personality overlay. - Keeps `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` native to Codex project-doc loading instead of duplicating them in OpenClaw prompt text, while fingerprinting their root workspace content so existing OpenClaw bindings rotate when the docs change. - Removes the lightweight Codex `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression so cron/lightweight Codex runs preserve exact user input without disabling native project docs. Lightweight exact-command runs also do not disable Codex's native personality just because `SOUL.md` exists on disk; the SOUL override applies only when SOUL context is actually delivered. - Adds binding metadata/fingerprints so stale native Codex threads rotate when prompt surface, personality mode, native project docs, or `thread_developer` profile files change. ## Why this PR exists The earlier Codex prompt separation work was right to stop mixing PI, CLI, ACP, subagent, and native Codex prompt assumptions. Native Codex should keep its own base/model contract, while OpenClaw should add only the host-runtime bridge it owns: message delivery, dynamic OpenClaw tools, ACP/OpenClaw delegation boundaries, and runtime/channel context. The next optimization moved `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `USER.md` out of every turn and into Codex developer instructions. That was attractive for token efficiency, but the adversarial review found a real quality/regression risk: users edit soul/profile/memory files during long-running OpenClaw sessions, and native Codex thread developer instructions are not a per-turn live-reload surface. Once a native Codex thread is already bound, resume-time developer/personality changes can be ignored by the loaded thread unless OpenClaw intentionally rotates the binding. The final direction here is: quality is the default, token efficiency is a choice. ## Prompt shape ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Codex base/model instructions"] --> B["Codex native personality slot"] B --> C["OpenClaw Codex developer bridge"] C --> D{"workspacePromptSurface"} D -->|"per_turn_context (default)"| E["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
MEMORY / active BOOTSTRAP
fresh current-turn context"] D -->|"thread_developer (opt-in)"| F["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
Codex thread developer instructions"] E --> G["Current user request"] F --> G H["AGENTS.md / AGENTS.override.md"] --> I["Codex native project-doc loader"] I --> G J["HEARTBEAT.md"] --> K["Heartbeat collaboration-mode pointer"] K --> G ``` The bridge text is intentionally narrow. It says OpenClaw is the host runtime and Codex is the native execution harness, that OpenClaw may prepend current-turn workspace/memory/skill/channel context, and that this context is reference material for the current turn rather than a replacement for Codex system/developer instructions. It also explicitly avoids treating every OpenClaw turn as coding work. When non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered and `personalityMode` is left at the default, Codex receives the protocol enum `personality: "none"`. This is not model-visible text that says "have no personality"; Codex's protocol describes `none` as an empty native personality placeholder. The positive personality source remains `SOUL.md` via the OpenClaw bridge. Empty placeholder SOUL files keep Codex's native personality behavior. ## What we learned ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Original legacy behavior
fresh per-turn soul/profile context"] --> B["Prompt separation
Codex gets Codex lane, PI gets PI lane"] B --> C["Token optimization
stable files moved to developerInstructions"] C --> D["Adversarial finding
developerInstructions are not reliable live-refresh for existing native threads"] D --> E["This PR
default legacy quality + opt-in thread developer mode + binding rotation"] ``` Key findings from the review: - `SOUL.md` is personality, not collaboration mode. `collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` is too strong and mode-oriented for general soul/profile content; it remains appropriate for heartbeat/cron pointer behavior. - `AGENTS.md` and `AGENTS.override.md` are Codex project-doc concerns. OpenClaw should not duplicate them into turn text, but it should stop suppressing native loading and rotate OpenClaw's native Codex binding when loaded project-doc content changes. - `MEMORY.md`, active `BOOTSTRAP.md`, and skills are volatile enough to remain per-turn context even in the token-efficient mode. - `thread_developer` needs freshness metadata. If users opt into it, profile-file edits must rotate the native Codex thread rather than silently relying on resume-time overrides. - The default should preserve the legacy quality/freshness behavior because token savings should not silently outweigh agent identity quality. OpenAI's Codex prompt write-up describes native project-doc loading with `AGENTS.override.md`, `AGENTS.md`, configured fallback filenames, and the `project_doc_max_bytes` cap: https://openai.com/index/unrolling-the-codex-agent-loop/ ## Authority and lifetime | Surface | Owner | Files / content | Lifetime | This PR | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Codex base/model instructions | Codex | Codex system/model stack | Native thread | OpenClaw does not override | | Codex personality slot | Codex protocol | `friendly`, `pragmatic`, `none` | Native thread/turn param | Configurable via `personalityMode`; defaults to `none` only when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered | | OpenClaw developer bridge | OpenClaw | Runtime/delivery/tool/delegation bridge | Native thread | Kept narrow and host-owned | | OpenClaw per-turn context | OpenClaw | Soul/profile/memory/bootstrap/skills/channel context | Every turn | Default quality-first surface | | Codex thread developer instructions | OpenClaw -> Codex | Stable soul/profile files only | Native thread | Opt-in `thread_developer`, with fingerprint rotation | | Native Codex project docs | Codex | `AGENTS.md`, `AGENTS.override.md`, fallbacks | Native project-doc load | Preserved; no `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression | | Heartbeat collaboration pointer | OpenClaw | Pointer to `HEARTBEAT.md` | Heartbeat turn | Still separate from soul/profile | ## Scenario coverage | Scenario | Expected behavior | | --- | --- | | Default Codex app-server run with non-empty `SOUL.md` | Stable profile files are current-turn context; Codex native personality is disabled; bridge points personality at `SOUL.md`. | | Default Codex app-server run with empty `SOUL.md` | Empty placeholder content does not suppress Codex's native personality overlay. | | User edits `SOUL.md` mid-session in default mode | Next turn sees the edit because the file remains per-turn context. | | User opts into `thread_developer` | Stable profile files move to developer instructions; `MEMORY.md` and active `BOOTSTRAP.md` remain turn context. | | User edits `SOUL.md` in `thread_developer` mode | OpenClaw fingerprints the tracked developer files and starts a fresh native Codex thread. | | User edits `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` | OpenClaw fingerprints loaded native project-doc content and rotates the native Codex binding. | | Lightweight cron Codex run | OpenClaw still skips extra bootstrap prompt text for exact command preservation, does not disable native Codex project docs, and does not send `personality: "none"` unless the user explicitly configured `personalityMode: "none"`. | | Existing stale binding from the previous PR | Missing prompt-surface metadata forces a new native Codex thread, so old bindings do not preserve stale developer/user context. | | Manual `/codex resume ` binding | The minimal manual binding resumes once, then persists the current prompt-surface/personality metadata after successful resume. | ## "native-codex", defaultWithoutDeliveredSoul: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: defaultConfig, hasSoulContext: false }) ?? "native-codex", forcedNoneWithoutDeliveredSoul: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: readCodexPluginConfig({ personalityMode: "none" }), hasSoulContext: false }) ?? "native-codex" }, null, 2));' ``` - Evidence after fix (terminal capture / copied live output): ```text run-attempt focused app-server path: Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 6 passed | 186 skipped (192) thread-lifecycle app-server path: Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 36 passed (36) { "proof": "openclaw codex workspace prompt routing", "head": "342c00b220", "defaultSurface": "per_turn_context", "threadSurface": "thread_developer", "defaultWithDeliveredSoul": "none", "defaultWithoutDeliveredSoul": "native-codex", "forcedNoneWithoutDeliveredSoul": "none" } ``` - Observed result after fix: The focused app-server tests exercise `runCodexAppServerAttempt` request construction against the mock Codex app-server.
